comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: STATUS (handoff): OPEN, NOT yet claimed/implemented. Plan REMEDIATED through 5 review rounds and the description now fully specifies the design (two-lane tier-tagged impact_code; CodeSeverityAttrs; trigger_likelihood NOT likelihood [avoids base-field collision]; reversibility_floor gated on impact_base>0 with concrete path-pattern surface detection; 3-part verify wiring). CRITICAL: this child references impact_fn from sibling fishable-apivorous-redhead (stack commit 29d9fa863) which is NOT on origin/main, so its plan-review MUST be run grounded against the stack: REBAR_GATE_REF=<stack tip 23581b171> rebar review-plan albite-lazy-barb (default origin/main grounding falsely reports impact_fn absent). Last review (round 5, stack-grounded) was down to advisory-only-or-near-pass; a round-5 result may be in job tmp rp_codeimpact5.txt. Impl plan: verify.py code_review_verification_model via shared _build_verification_output; decide.impact_code; code_review/contracts.py register code_review_verification; code-review.yaml verify output_schema + code-review-verify.md outputs: front-matter -> code_review_verification; code_review_decide DET-enriches attrs (churn via git, reversibility surface via paths) + passes impact_fn=impact_code; fixture tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl; test_impact_code.py; ADR under docs/adr/.

compiled_state.description:
# Code-review impact model redesign: two-lane tier-tagged MAX with re-anchored scale

## Why
Production-severity axes + the mean structurally mis-measure code-review's maintainability/latent-regression
findings: a feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores 0.415 because prod_impact is 'none' and
reversibility 'easy' drag it down. Landmines and nits overlap so no scalar threshold separates them.

## What
A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model (`decide.impact_code`) dispatched per-gate off the
shared `decide.pass3_decide` via the `impact_fn` param (that param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the
plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead; code-review now passes `impact_fn=impact_code`).
- TIER VALUES: each consequence binary carries the severity it implies if true — minor=0.3, moderate=0.6,
  serious=0.9. Lane-severity = MAX of the tiers of the TRUE binaries (no dilution; a minor/moderate binary
  cannot alone reach the block zone). No cross-binary compounding (conservative default).
- TWO LANES: `prod_lane = MAX(correctness tier-tagged binaries) * trigger_likelihood_mult`; `maint_lane =
  MAX(maintainability tier-tagged binaries) * freq_mult`. Each binary is assigned to EXACTLY one lane + one
  tier by an explicit table (below).
- TRIGGER-LIKELIHOOD (LLM-emitted enum on the NEW field `trigger_likelihood`, deliberately NOT named
  `likelihood` — the base SeverityAttrs already declares `likelihood`=low|medium|high mapped by _LIKE01, so a
  distinct name avoids a field-override collision): `common=1.0 / sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25`; absent =>
  `common` (1.0) so a serious correctness binary is not silently dampened by missing metadata.
- CHANGE-FREQUENCY MULTIPLIER: `freq_mult = 0.5 + 0.5 * min(churn_90d, 30) / 30` where churn_90d = commits
  touching the finding's file in the last 90 days (`git log`, DET). Linear, saturates at 30. Applies to
  maint_lane only. FALLBACK: git unavailable / shallow clone / no file attributable => churn_90d = 0 =>
  freq_mult = 0.5 (the minimum). Best-effort; never fails the review.
- REVERSIBILITY FLOOR (det/hybrid), GATED: `reversibility_floor = 0.6` iff BOTH (a) `impact_base > 0` (the
  finding identifies at least one true consequence) AND (b) the finding's file is a HARD-TO-REVERSE SURFACE by
  these DET path rules (else 0): migration = a path segment `migrations/` or a segment starting `migration`;
  released-artifact/packaging = basename in {pyproject.toml, setup.py, setup.cfg} or path contains `CHANGELOG`;
  serialization/schema = extension in {.proto, .sql} or basename matching `schema*.json`/`*.schema.json`;
  deletion = the file is removed in the diff (only `-` lines / git status D). 'exported public API' is
  best-effort (undetectable from paths alone -> treated as NOT hard-to-reverse; conservative floor 0).
  The `impact_base > 0` gate is deliberate: it lifts a GENUINE defect on a one-way-door surface to >=0.6 but
  does NOT manufacture impact for a clean/no-consequence finding on the same file (fixes the "fires
  unconditionally, over-inflates nits" hole).
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER: `amp = 1.0 if (silent_failure or escapes_automation) else 0.8`. Both are LLM-emitted
  booleans in `code_review_verification_model` (a code-review detection judgment), defaulting to false (=>
  amp 0.8) when absent.
- EXPLICIT COMPOSE ORDER (single expression, no ambiguity):
  `impact_base = MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane); impact = max(min(1.0, impact_base * amp), reversibility_floor)`.
- RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor; co-calibrate tier values + block threshold on the labeled set
  (below) so a maintainability landmine reaches ~0.8+ and a nit ~0.15-0.2.
- CARVED SUB-QUESTION SET (audit-approved) with EXPLICIT lane + tier (ADD to, do NOT replace, the existing
  severity_attributes — CodeSeverityAttrs extends the base five):
    prod lane:  data_loss_without_recovery=0.9, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere=0.9,
                silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision=0.9, capability_degraded=0.6
    maint lane: unversioned_published_contract_break=0.9, safety_net_removal_without_replacement=0.9,
                contract_drift=0.6, hidden_invariant=0.6, implicit_coupling=0.3, dead_code=0.3
  Each serious binary carries its charitable carve-out in its verify-prompt definition.

## Implementation mechanics + calibration data
- SUB-ANSWER SCHEMA: emit the tier-tagged binaries (bool) + `trigger_likelihood` (enum: common|sometimes|rare) + `silent_failure` /
  `escapes_automation` (bool) as NEW fields on a `code_review_verification_model()` whose severity_attributes
  is `CodeSeverityAttrs` EXTENDING the shared 5 attrs — a SINGLE aggregate Pass-2 verify call, no added LLM
  calls. Wired in THREE named places: (i) register a `code_review_verification` contract in
  `code_review/contracts.py` -> code_review_verification_model; (ii) change the verify step's `output_schema`
  in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` (~line 147) from `verification` to
  `code_review_verification`; (iii) update the `outputs:` front-matter key in
  `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md` from `verification` to `code_review_verification`. The
  kernel `verification` contract (plan-review + default) is untouched. Each new binary defaults false / trigger_likelihood 'common' /
  detection false, so an older/absent verifier ABSTAINS (no true binary => lane 0 => impact 0).
- VERIFY-PROMPT INSTRUCTION TEXT (required so the LLM knows how to populate the new fields): `code-review-verify.md`
  gains an ADDITIVE 'CODE-REVIEW CONSEQUENCE BINARIES' block (kept alongside the existing severity-attribute
  instructions) that names each of the 10 tier-tagged binaries under its lane with its tier (serious/moderate/
  minor) and a one-line 'set TRUE only when the change genuinely exhibits this' rule, plus a 'TRIGGER LIKELIHOOD +
  DETECTION' block defining `trigger_likelihood` (common|sometimes|rare, default common) and the `silent_failure` /
  `escapes_automation` booleans. It instructs the verifier to leave a binary FALSE unless earned (a false binary
  contributes nothing; a minor/moderate binary alone cannot reach the block zone), mirroring the additive axes
  block plan-review's `plan_review_verifier.md` gained.
- DET ENRICHMENT (merge target = the VERIFICATION dict, NOT the finding dict): `pass3_decide` reads its
  attrs from the *verification* dict (`verification.get("severity_attributes")`), NOT from the finding
  dict (in `pass3_over_findings`, the finding `f` is used only for `threshold_for(f.get("criteria"))`;
  the attrs `impact_code` receives come from `verifs.get(i)`). So `code_review_decide` computes `churn90`
  (90-day git commit count for the finding's file) + `hard_to_reverse_surface` (from the changed-file
  path / deletion) per finding and injects them IN PLACE into `reshape.verifications[i]["severity_attributes"]`
  (the exact dict `impact_code` receives) BEFORE calling `pass3_over_findings`. Writing to the finding's
  own `severity_attributes` would silently no-op (impact_code would read only defaults). Best-effort: any
  git/path failure leaves the signal at its safe default (churn 0 => freq_mult 0.5; surface False => no floor).
- PER-GATE DISPATCH: `code_review_decide` passes `impact_fn=impact_code`. Plan-review is byte-unchanged
  (it threads impact_plan; nothing here touches it) — asserted by test.
- CALIBRATION/LABELED SET: a curated fixture at a NEW `tests/unit/fixtures/` directory (created by this ticket, with a provenance README) — `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` — of
  code-review findings each tagged HIGH (block-worthy) or NIT, provenance = the dogfooded code-review corpus +
  this program's held-out adjudication (initial ~31 labeled findings; grows via the data-capture child).
- ADR: this novel two-lane model is an architectural decision — record it under `docs/adr/`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (`impact_code`), `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`
  (`code_review_verification_model` + CodeSeverityAttrs), `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` (verify-step contract
  wiring, the DET enrichment in the decide op, the verify prompt), the labeled fixture + re-anchoring
  calibration test, and the ADR. Plan-review impact unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `impact_code = max(min(1.0, MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane) * amp), reversibility_floor)` per the explicit
      compose above, with tier-tagged-binary MAX within each lane using the stated tier values and the stated
      trigger_likelihood_mult / freq_mult / reversibility_floor (impact_base>0 gated) / amp formulas. Unit-tested for
      each multiplier + the gate. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_code.py -v`.
- [ ] Carved serious/moderate/minor sub-question set implemented with charitable carve-outs, ADDED to (not
      replacing) severity_attributes via CodeSeverityAttrs; emitted in ONE aggregate Pass-2 call (no added
      LLM calls); reversibility/reach/change-frequency are DET/hybrid (diff + git) with a churn=0/freq=0.5
      fallback when git is unavailable. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_code.py -v`.
- [ ] Per-gate dispatch via `impact_fn=impact_code`; a test asserts plan-review's decide path is unchanged
      (impact_plan still dispatched; impact_code never touches it); an ADR under docs/adr/ records the model.
      Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_code.py tests/unit/test_impact_plan.py -v`.
- [ ] `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` exists (each row HIGH/NIT, provenance in a header
      comment or sibling README). A test asserts impact_code SEPARATES the classes on the fixture with a
      CONCRETE margin (NO "or explained" escape): `median(impact_code[HIGH]) - median(impact_code[NIT]) >=
      0.30`, AND that this median gap is strictly GREATER than the old mean `impact`'s gap on the same
      fixture (the redesign beats the old scalar). Validated diff-grounded before any threshold-down tuning
      (the tuning itself is the calibration child raptorial-galloping-dragon).
compiled_state.title: Code-review impact model redesign: two-lane tier-tagged MAX with re-anchored scale
